proposed_claim: The artefact classifies healthcare accountability facts as portable referenced committed or excluded.
minimum_evidence: closed field registry; frozen hero-case accountability questions; field-classification matrix; field-deletion or necessity results; privacy review
current_evidence: C3 necessity audit; 35-row HIE boundary matrix; closed HIE schemas; complete synthetic hero case; semantic and payload-exclusion checks
allowed_wording_now: The executed synthetic disclosure case instantiated the reported four-class evidence/custody boundary for its declared accountability questions.
allowed_wording_after_gate: The executed hero case supported the reported four-class evidence/custody boundary under the declared accountability questions.
prohibited_wording: universally complete field model; expert-validated boundary; legal minimisation proof

proposed_claim: A complete synthetic cross-organisational disclosure case traverses source context envelope FHIR projection local receipt and verification.

current_evidence: retained HIE-DISCLOSURE-001 source Bundle; Consent version 3; policy version 6; decision D-204; DiagnosticReport version 2; signed envelope; local A2 receipt; verification report; FHIR projection; manifests
allowed_wording_now: The complete synthetic disclosure case traversed the executed Route C reference pipeline; it was not an operational patient exchange.
allowed_wording_after_gate: The complete synthetic disclosure case traversed the executed Route C reference pipeline.
prohibited_wording: real patient case; operational hospital exchange; clinical validation

proposed_claim: The reported Route C FHIR examples pass the declared official FHIR R4 and applicable BALP validation toolchain.
minimum_evidence: one canonical minimal IG; pinned FHIR and BALP packages; SUSHI; IG Publisher; HL7 Validator; positive and negative examples; warning triage
current_evidence: retained SUSHI log; compact local IG package; IG Publisher QA with zero errors and 33 adjudicated warnings; four positive Validator units with zero fatal/error findings; two intended negative rejections; tool versions and JAR digests
allowed_wording_now: The exact C3 corpus passed the declared SUSHI IG Publisher and HL7 FHIR Validator pathway against FHIR R4 4.0.1 the local IG package and applicable BALP 1.1.4 profiles.
allowed_wording_after_gate: The reported examples passed the specified toolchain against the declared Route C profiles and applicable BALP profiles.
prohibited_wording: FHIR compliant; IHE certified; universal conformance; clinical interoperability proven

proposed_claim: The issuer signature is computed over deterministic RFC 8785 application-profile bytes.
minimum_evidence: application-profile rules; positive and negative canonicalisation vectors; deterministic reruns; release-candidate tests
current_evidence: C1 baseline canonicalisation tests; C3 exact canonical envelope bytes and digest; C4 byte-deterministic security corpus and repeated result equality on the working branch; final v2.2.0 candidate rerun remains C6
allowed_wording_now: The working branch reproduced deterministic TE application-profile bytes for the retained case and C4 corpus; the public v2.2.0 release claim remains unavailable until C6.
allowed_wording_after_gate: The v2.2.0 release candidate reproduced the canonical bytes and rejected the reported unsupported inputs.
prohibited_wording: new canonicalisation algorithm; universal FHIR canonical form; cross-language interoperability unless independently tested

proposed_claim: Controlled mutations to signed semantic fields are detected by issuer-signature verification under the test trust configuration.
minimum_evidence: field-substitution corpus; wrong-key and signature-byte mutations; exact signed-byte definition; zero false accepts
current_evidence: C4 retained actor organisation role Consent policy decision outcome resource provenance signature-byte unknown-key and wrong-key cases; all final expected issuer-side rejections passed; three authorised-issuer truth limitations were accepted and reported
allowed_wording_now: All final registered unauthorised issuer-side mutations were rejected under the deterministic test key registry; valid re-signing by an authorised issuer remained acceptable and therefore did not establish statement truth.
allowed_wording_after_gate: The release candidate reproduced the final C4 issuer-signature decisions under the declared trust configuration.
prohibited_wording: clinical truth; identity proofing; Consent or policy truth; legal non-repudiation; post-compromise security

proposed_claim: A nonce-based commitment detects changes to the committed source payload when the verifier has the exact candidate payload and nonce.
minimum_evidence: documented byte framing; nonce handling; payload and nonce mutations; no nonce in portable/public artefacts; privacy scan
current_evidence: C4 retained one positive candidate and six expected rejections covering payload bytes alternate JSON serialisation nonce representation profile context and short nonce; test nonce and declared clinical source markers were absent from inspected portable artefacts
allowed_wording_now: For the exact synthetic case the retained commitment verified only for the declared candidate bytes framing and nonce; the reported substitutions were rejected and no encryption claim is made.
allowed_wording_after_gate: The release candidate reproduced the C4 commitment and portable-custody decisions.
prohibited_wording: encryption; confidentiality by hashing; proof of semantic or clinical correctness; operational nonce secrecy

proposed_claim: The project-specific local A2 verifier checks receipt binding inclusion and retained-checkpoint consistency under its explicit policy.
minimum_evidence: algorithm audit; receipt schema; wrong index size root path key and checkpoint mutations; state non-advancement after failure
current_evidence: C4 retained receipt proof key replay rollback fork consistency and state-advancement cases; 54 final expected rejections across the complete corpus produced zero false accepts; eight rejected state cases left the checkpoint unchanged; six limitation acceptances include authorised issuer claims stateless split view authorised alternative tree-size assertion and exact replay
allowed_wording_now: The final C4 registry rejected its expected unauthorised and inconsistent cases under the local retained-checkpoint policy, while explicitly accepting the registered authorised-signer tree-size split-view and replay limitations.
allowed_wording_after_gate: The release candidate reproduced the final C4 receipt and retained-state decisions under the declared local policy.
prohibited_wording: RFC 9942 receipt; SCITT conformance; public transparency service; universal non-equivocation; truthful tree size; completeness; replay prevention

proposed_claim: No field forbidden by the declared Route C privacy profile is present in the inspected portable artefacts and logs.

current_evidence: C3 semantic report found no forbidden resource type no declared clinical-value path and no dangling local source reference; C4 found neither the deterministic test nonce nor declared clinical source markers in the inspected envelope and portable Bundle; release-wide archive scan remains C6
allowed_wording_now: The executed C3-C4 scans found no declared forbidden clinical field test nonce or declared source marker in the inspected portable case artefacts; this is not a general privacy guarantee.
allowed_wording_after_gate: The release-wide scans identified no declared forbidden fields or secrets in the inspected Route C distribution.
